DTEval 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Changelog

    Fit Diffusion Tube Models

    fit.tube.Rd

    Functions for fitting diffusion tube (DT) data calculations using DTEval.

    fitTubeModel(
  data,
  tube = ".value",
  inputs = NULL,
  by = NULL,
  model = NULL,
  simplify = FALSE,
  min.count = -1,
  min.prop = -1,
  new.data = NULL,
  ...
)

fitTubeModel_gam(data, tube, inputs, new.data = NULL, ...)

fitTubeModel_loess(data, tube, inputs, new.data = NULL, ...)

    Arguments

    data

    Data source, typically a data.frame or similar, containing data-series of diffusion tube records.

    tube

    The name of the data-series (in data) to fit a model to (see model), typically the DT NO2 concentrations (in ug/m3).

    inputs

    The name(s) of the model inputs.

    by

    The name(s) of hierarchical grouping terms. These are used to sub-sample the data when build multiple models.

    model

    The model to fit, by default fitTubeModel_loess

    simplify

    Option to simplify the data before fitting by averaging common inputs, default FALSE.

    min.count

    The minimum data count required for a model build. The default, -1, disables this option.

    min.prop

    The minimum proportion of non-NA data required for a model build. The default, -1, disables this option and values higher than 1 exclude all supplied data.

    new.data

    The type of prediction to generate. The default, NULL, returns the supplied data with an extra column of the requested predictions added as [tube].pred. The alternative option 'input.ranges' generates a data.frame of the model input ranges.

    ...

    additional arguments, currently passed on to model and used if recognised.

    Value

    All functions return a data.frame of requested data statistics.

    Details

    fitTubeModel attempts to fit a model to the supplied inputs.

    It and related functions assume that data is data DTEval will recognise as Diffusion Tube data, so either previously tagged tube data or data that is tag-able using a default call of tagTube

    Examples

    if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
#basic examples
mod <- fitTubeModel(dt.brd, inputs=c(".latitude", ".longitude"),
                    by =".year", simplify = T)
tubePlot(mod, ".value", ".value.pred", facet=".year")
} # }

    Contents
